proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65 — plausibly age in years) is positively associated with pfs_months: each additional unit of feature_078 is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate OLS: feature_078 has a strong positive linear association with pfs_months. Each unit increase = +0.174 months PFS. R^2 alone = 0.732. Mean PFS climbs monotonically across age bins from 0.09 mo (30-40) to 7.20 mo (80-90).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (3-level ordinal: 0/1/2) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher categories (likely a stage- or performance-status-like variable) have shorter PFS than category 0.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_057')['pfs_months'].mean(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate mean PFS is 4.68 / 3.49 / 2.38 months at levels 0/1/2. After adjusting for feature_078, level 1 vs 0 = -1.18 mo, level 2 vs 0 = -2.29 mo. Strongly significant in expected direction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (continuous, range 0.12-3622, highly right-skewed — plausibly a lab value such as PSA or LDH) is negatively associated with pfs_months on the log scale: higher log(feature_013) predicts shorter PFS.

analyses.code: df['log_f013'] = np.log(df['feature_013']); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After adjusting for feature_078 and feature_057, log(feature_013) coefficient = -0.349 mo per log-unit. Highly significant negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (continuous, 0-24.6, mean 3.8) is negatively associated with pfs_months after adjusting for feature_078, feature_057, and feature_013.
feature_009 (continuous, 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8 — plausibly serum albumin) is positively associated with pfs_months after adjusting for the above predictors.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_006 coefficient = -0.077 (highly significant negative); feature_009 coefficient = +0.479 (highly significant positive). Both directions consistent with hypotheses. Adjusted R^2 = 0.969.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 (race: asian/black/hispanic/other/white) is associated with pfs_months: at least one race category differs in mean PFS from the others.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_018')['pfs_months'].mean(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_018)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate mean PFS by race: asian 3.80, black 3.74, hispanic 3.74, other 3.75, white 3.74. Differences are tiny (<0.07 mo) and none significant (all p > 0.14). Refuted: race is not associated with PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_045 (insurance: medicare/medicaid/private/uninsured) is associated with pfs_months: at least one insurance category differs in mean PFS from the others.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_045')['pfs_months'].mean(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + C(feature_045)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by insurance: medicaid 3.74, medicare 3.75, private 3.74, uninsured 3.73. After full adjustment all insurance category coefficients have |est| < 0.001 and p > 0.9. Refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (5-level ordinal taking values 6/7/8/9/10 — plausibly Gleason score) is negatively associated with pfs_months as a main effect: higher Gleason predicts shorter PFS.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_067')['pfs_months'].mean(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + C(feature_067)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate mean PFS is essentially flat across Gleason levels (3.70-3.76). Even after multivariable adjustment, Gleason 8/9/10 vs 6 contribute only -0.025/-0.024/-0.020 months — statistically significant only because of the very large N. The clinically expected strong main effect of Gleason is essentially absent here.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary 0/1 features, after adjusting for the established main predictors (feature_078, feature_057, log_f013, feature_006, feature_009), feature_109 (prevalence ~10%) has a non-zero main effect on pfs_months.
Among the binary features, feature_039 (prevalence ~10%) has a non-zero main effect on pfs_months after the same adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_109', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_109 main-effect coefficient = +0.152 months, p < 1e-180. Apparent positive effect (supports h9) — but see iteration 10: this is driven entirely by patients who are also feature_039=1.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_039', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_039 main-effect coefficient = +0.149 months, p < 1e-176. Apparent positive effect (supports h10) — but see iteration 10: this is driven entirely by patients who are also feature_109=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 and feature_039 act independently and additively on pfs_months: their combined effect equals the sum of their individual main effects.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_109 * feature_039', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: When the interaction is included, both main effects collapse to ~0 (feature_109 = -0.0001, p=0.98; feature_039 = -0.0001, p=0.98) and the interaction term feature_109:feature_039 = +1.437 mo, p effectively 0. Hypothesis of additive independent action is REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a strong positive synergistic interaction between feature_109 and feature_039: only patients with both feature_109=1 AND feature_039=1 derive a substantial PFS benefit, consistent with a biomarker-targeted-therapy pattern (one variable is the therapy, the other is the eligibility biomarker).

analyses.code: df_x = df.loc[df['feature_109']==1]; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_039', data=df_x).fit(); ditto for df.loc[df['feature_109']==0]
analyses.result_summary: Stratified analysis: among feature_109=1 patients (n=4,996), feature_039 effect = +1.436 mo (p<1e-300). Among feature_109=0 patients (n=45,004), feature_039 effect = -0.0002 mo (p=0.96). Effect of feature_039 is exclusive to the feature_109=1 stratum, exactly the pattern expected for a biomarker-defined responder population.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The synergistic feature_109 × feature_039 PFS benefit is uniform across Gleason levels (feature_067).

analyses.code: sub['gleason_high']=(sub['feature_067']>=8).astype(int); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ... + feature_109 * feature_039 * gleason_high', data=sub).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Within the responder subgroup (feature_109=1, feature_039=1, n=529), Gleason 8/9/10 vs 6 are -2.90/-2.87/-2.84 mo. The 3-way interaction feature_109:feature_039:gleason_high = -2.80 mo (p effectively 0): the +2.82 mo benefit observed in low-grade tumors (Gleason 6-7) is essentially eliminated in high-grade (Gleason >=8) tumors. Uniform-effect hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The synergistic feature_109 × feature_039 PFS benefit is also modified by feature_043 (binary, prevalence ~20%): patients with feature_043=1 derive less benefit from the feature_109=1 / feature_039=1 combination than patients with feature_043=0.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ... + feature_109 * feature_039 * feature_043', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_109:feature_039:feature_043 3-way interaction = -1.72 mo (p effectively 0). Marginal main effects of feature_043 and its 2-way interactions with feature_109 or feature_039 are all near zero. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: feature_043=1 attenuates the responder benefit by ~1.7 mo (about 60% of the 2.82-mo Gleason-low benefit).

proposed_hypotheses.text: When the 3-way modifiers (gleason_high and feature_043) are jointly included, the feature_109 × feature_039 benefit is concentrated in patients with Gleason 6-7 AND feature_043=0; in this subgroup the benefit is approximately 3 months PFS.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ... + responder + responder:gleason_high + responder:feature_043', data=sub).fit() where responder = feature_109 & feature_039
analyses.result_summary: Final integrated model: responder coefficient = +3.09 mo (p~0); responder:gleason_high = -2.76 mo; responder:feature_043 = -1.62 mo. Net benefit ~ +3.1 mo only when Gleason<=7 AND feature_043=0; reduced to ~ +0.3 mo if Gleason>=8 alone, ~ +1.5 mo if feature_043=1 alone, ~negative if both modifiers present. Adjusted R^2 = 0.980.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of the candidate treatment (feature_109=1) differs by race (feature_018): some race groups have lower likelihood of receiving feature_109 than others (an access disparity).
Biomarker positivity (feature_039=1) differs by race (feature_018).

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_018')['feature_109'].mean(); chi2 test
analyses.result_summary: feature_109 prevalence by race: asian 0.107, black 0.094, hispanic 0.100, other 0.097, white 0.100. Range ~1.3 percentage points; differences are small and not clinically meaningful. No access disparity by race.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_018')['feature_039'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: feature_039 prevalence by race: asian 0.106, black 0.099, hispanic 0.099, other 0.086, white 0.103. Differences are <2 percentage points and statistically and clinically negligible.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients in the responder subgroup (feature_109=1 AND feature_039=1), the magnitude of PFS benefit from being a responder differs by race.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ... + responder * C(feature_018)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratified: responder effect (vs non-responder) is +1.61 (asian, n_resp=30), +1.36 (black, n_resp=58), +1.43 (white, n_resp=355), +1.39 (hispanic, n_resp=78), +1.94 (other, n_resp=8). Interaction terms p=0.003-0.01 vs asian reference. Differences (~0.2 mo) are statistically detectable but small relative to the overall ~1.5-mo benefit and small subgroup sizes; not clearly clinically meaningful.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among responders, the PFS benefit differs by insurance type (feature_045): private and medicare patients derive smaller benefit than medicaid or uninsured.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ... + responder * C(feature_045)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratified responder effect: medicaid +1.59, medicare +1.47, private +1.32, uninsured +1.56. Interaction terms: medicare-vs-medicaid -0.11 (p=0.009); private-vs-medicaid -0.26 (p=1.4e-9); uninsured-vs-medicaid -0.03 (p=0.73). Statistically significant private-vs-medicaid difference but absolute magnitude (~0.26 mo) is small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (binary, prevalence ~55%) is negatively associated with pfs_months even after adjustment for the established main predictors.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_051 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate r=-0.13 with PFS but after full adjustment, feature_051 coefficient = -0.0018 mo, p=0.64. The univariate signal is fully accounted for by other covariates (likely log_f013/feature_006). Refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_109 × feature_039 PFS benefit is moderated by stage (feature_057): the absolute benefit is largest in the most advanced stage (level 2).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ... + responder * C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Responder main effect = +1.49 mo at stage 0 (reference). Interaction responder:stage1 = -0.11 (p=0.0003); responder:stage2 = +0.03 (p=0.42). Effect of being a responder is essentially the same at all 3 stages (range 1.39-1.52 mo). Hypothesis of larger benefit at advanced stage is NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other 5-level ordinal features (feature_016, feature_071, feature_096, feature_033, feature_064) are NOT significantly associated with pfs_months after adjustment for the established main predictors.

analyses.code: for c in ['feature_016','feature_071','feature_096','feature_033','feature_064']: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ ... + C({c})', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All 5-level ordinal features show non-significant adjusted coefficients (all |est| <= 0.013 mo, most p>0.1). They appear to be administrative or weakly informative features rather than prognostic clinical scores. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 (11-level integer, 0-10 — plausibly a comorbidity count or care-utilization index) is NOT associated with pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + C(feature_026)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All 10 dummy coefficients have |est| < 0.014 mo and p > 0.1. Hypothesis SUPPORTED — feature_026 contributes nothing after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After accounting for the feature_109 × feature_039 interaction and its modifiers, no additional binary feature has a residual main effect on pfs_months larger than ~0.05 mo.

analyses.code: Compute residuals from final model; t-test residuals vs each of 76 remaining binary features.
analyses.result_summary: Among 76 binary features, the largest residual mean differences are feature_027 (+0.024 mo), feature_043 (-0.016 mo), feature_121 (+0.015 mo). All <0.025 mo in magnitude. Hypothesis SUPPORTED — no additional clinically meaningful binary main effects remain. (feature_043's signal is fully explained by its 3-way interaction with feature_109:feature_039 from iteration 12.)

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triple interaction feature_109 × feature_039 × race (feature_018) is non-zero: race modifies the responder PFS benefit.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ... + feature_109 * feature_039 * C(feature_018)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Triple interaction estimates (vs asian reference): black -0.24 (p=0.003), hispanic -0.22 (p=0.004), white -0.18 (p=0.007), other +0.31 (p=0.027). Detectable but the small subgroup sizes (asian responders n=30, other n=8) make these unstable; magnitudes are modest relative to the +1.4-mo overall responder benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous lab-like predictors feature_092, feature_014, feature_070, feature_044, feature_084, feature_028, feature_120, feature_054, feature_062, feature_031, feature_082 (vital-sign / lab range), once adjusted for the main 5-predictor model, contribute negligibly to pfs_months (each |partial r| < 0.05).

analyses.code: Compute partial correlations of every continuous feature with PFS residuals from main 5-predictor model.
analyses.result_summary: Top partial correlations after main adjustment: feature_092 r=-0.041, feature_014 r=-0.013, feature_070 r=+0.008, feature_044 r=-0.001 ... All other continuous features |r| < 0.05. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: these vital-sign / chemistry features carry essentially no additional prognostic information for PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 carries information that is informative for PFS modeling.

analyses.code: df['feature_007'].value_counts()
analyses.result_summary: feature_007 is constant (= 0 for all 50,000 patients). It carries no information. Hypothesis trivially refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete prognostic model — feature_078 (linear), feature_057 (3-level), log(feature_013), feature_006, feature_009, plus the responder = (feature_109=1 AND feature_039=1) interaction with its Gleason-high and feature_043 modifiers — explains the great majority of variance in pfs_months and leaves only minor residual heterogeneity.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + responder + responder:gleason_high + responder:feature_043', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final integrated model adjusted R^2 = 0.980. All terms highly significant in expected directions. Each year of feature_078 = +0.174 mo PFS; feature_057 levels 0/1/2 differ by 1.18 and 2.29 mo; log(feature_013) = -0.349 mo/log-unit; feature_006 = -0.077 mo/unit; feature_009 = +0.479 mo/unit; responder benefit = +3.09 mo, eliminated by Gleason >=8 (-2.76) or attenuated by feature_043=1 (-1.62). Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
